role_title: Programs and Business Operations Lead, Cities

location.office_time_required: occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager

level_signals: Regularly interfaces with and influences senior management
Leads strategic development and execution of highly scalable programs across multiple jurisdictions
Base pay range $168K–$203K consistent with senior IC at a large tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in regulatory operations, legal/policy compliance, consulting, project management, or related fields, with a strong ability to leverage data and patterns to scale programs effectively.
Ability to navigate ambiguity and lead multiple projects in a fast-paced and complex regulatory environment.
Experience building scalable technical solutions and processes to mitigate risks.
Experience designing and operationalizing regulatory workflows.
Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex information as applicable and required for varied audiences.
Comfortable and skilled at regularly interfacing with, influencing and being a thought partner to senior management to inform strategy and decision-making, yielding positive outcomes.
Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills, with a track record of aligning cross-functional teams to achieve business objectives.

scope.surface: Cities team — global regulatory compliance programs and operations across multiple jurisdictions
scope.business_model: platform / marketplace (B2C and B2B, two-sided host-guest marketplace)
primary_metrics_inferred: Alignment of cross-functional teams on regulatory objectives
core_responsibilities: Lead the strategic development and execution of highly scalable programs across multiple jurisdictions
Build and maintain program management frameworks that ensure measurable outcomes and sustainable operations
Translate emerging regulatory trends, data, and cross-functional inputs into actionable business strategies for senior leadership
Influence cross-functional roadmaps and corporate strategy through deep expertise in regulatory frameworks
Drive high-impact strategic projects aimed at enhancing efficiency and effectiveness
Analyze data to identify trends, surface anomalies, and perform the necessary investigation

compensation.equity_form: bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits mentioned

green_flags: Clear team mandate and strategic scope (Cities regulatory operations)
Employee Travel Credits perk — relevant to Airbnb's mission
red_flags: 10+ years required is a hard floor that eliminates many candidates
Remote-US only with state exclusions — exact excluded states not listed in JD; candidates must verify eligibility
No visa sponsorship information explicitly stated; work auth left unclear
No team size or direct reports information provided

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years required + leadership signals)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
scope.cross_functional_partners (partially inferred; JD mentions cross-functional broadly without naming specific teams)
location.work_auth (partially inferred; JD says must live in eligible state implying US-only but no explicit sponsorship statement)
ambiguities: Visa sponsorship and work authorization policy not explicitly stated in the JD; work_auth set to no-sponsorship inferred from 'must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity' language which implies US-based restriction, but this is not a definitive statement on sponsorship.
manages_people is not stated; set to false as no direct reports or people management language appears, but 'lead with influence' language is used throughout.
The role title says 'Lead' which could be a senior IC or a people manager role at Airbnb; treated as senior IC given no explicit people management responsibility mentioned.
